_risk_level_doc: Change is additive — new branch inside changelog-analyst only. Non-precommitted path is entirely unaffected. No shared infrastructure, no schema changes, no auth boundary changes. Two affected files; blast radius is narrow.
score_injection_action: Ranked Disgraced with range <0 and a recent close_fail_qa_pass penalty: taking extra care to keep spec scope tight (additive-only changes, no scope creep), ensure every AC is concretely testable, and produce precise multi-repo grant handling instructions that QA can verify mechanically — all to avoid the kind of QA-pass/close-fail gap that caused the -5 penalty in do-20260529-210815.

requirement.original: /dev 经常会出现一个可以改进的地方，就是其他session不小心把这个session的内容--bulk commit了，这个session commit command运行的时候就会被block。现在为commit自动加入应对这个的方式，就是对自己这一轮开发的内容覆盖新的commit（不动之前bulk的）
requirement.clarified: When another session's --bulk run pre-empts this session's files before /commit <task-id> runs, changelog-analyst returns nothing_to_commit_precommitted and the session is blocked (no push-gate token). Add a recovery path in agents/changelog-analyst.md that detects this state and creates a git commit --allow-empty with task attribution, writes the push-gate token, and returns commit_status: committed. Must not modify the prior bulk commit.
requirement.what: Recovery commit path in changelog-analyst's nothing_to_commit_precommitted detection
requirement.why: Session is blocked from /push when another session's bulk commit accidentally picks up its files — no push-gate token is written, no task-attributed commit exists
where: commands/commit.md (Step 5 only — write two grants for multi-repo coverage)

included: Recovery path in agents/changelog-analyst.md for nothing_to_commit_precommitted detection (BULK=false only)
Range scan (baseline_head_sha..HEAD) for pre-empted commits instead of HEAD-only
Push-gate token write after recovery commit
commands/commit.md Step 5: write second commit grant for multi-repo recovery
excluded: Modifying or reverting prior bulk commits
New scripts or Python helper files
CAS ancestor check on push-gate token (DO NOT rule 7 sufficient)

success_criteria: After /commit <task-id> where task files were pre-empted by bulk, push-gate token exists with recovery commit SHA
Recovery commit subject contains task-id, body contains Precommitted-by sha(s) and Attributed-files
git log shows prior bulk commit untouched at HEAD~1
Normal (non-precommitted) path behavior is byte-for-byte identical
constraints: git commit --allow-empty -F <tmpfile> with mktemp + trap (DO NOT rule 12)
No git add -A or git add . (DO NOT rule 1)
DO NOT rule 7: skip push-gate write if existing token session_id differs from PUSH_GATE_SID
Changelog-analyst MUST NOT invoke write-commit-grant.py — grant pre-written by /commit Step 5
Recovery commit subject must not match forbidden regex patterns (DO NOT rule 10)

path_dependent_shared_infra: scripts/write-commit-grant.py (read-only: called from commit.md Step 5; no changes to script itself)
hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py (read-only: grant consumed by guard; no changes)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: After another session's --bulk run picks up this session's files, /commit <task-id> finds nothing to commit and returns nothing_to_commit_precommitted. No push-gate token is written. /push is blocked.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: The nothing_to_commit_precommitted detection branch in agents/changelog-analyst.md leads directly to a status return — no recovery commit is created, no push-gate token written. The session ends with a valid-but-unused commit grant and no task attribution.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — gap in existing spec prose, not a regression introduced by a specific commit
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The nothing_to_commit_precommitted status was designed as an informational return (bulk already handled it); the blocking consequence on /push was not addressed.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: No push-gate token means /push is hard-blocked. No task-attributed commit means the work is recorded only under the bulk session's imprecise attribution, losing the Task-id chain.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Present since nothing_to_commit_precommitted detection was added
observations: agents/changelog-analyst.md Structured Final Status Output section: nothing_to_commit_precommitted detection ends at 'return this status' with no subsequent Phase 10 push-gate write (inferred: read agents/changelog-analyst.md lines 590-668)
commands/commit.md Step 5 writes exactly one single-use commit grant for BULK=false (inferred: read commands/commit.md lines 79-94)

pretool-git-privilege-guard.py _lock_grant_for_posttool is called only on git commit Bash invocation; an empty working tree triggers no git commit call, so the grant is not consumed (inferred: read hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py lines 272-297, 546-589)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — no live session replay available)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: User description + code reading: the three-step SHA-stable check in agents/changelog-analyst.md returns nothing_to_commit_precommitted with no recovery path. Phase 10 push-gate write is only reached after a successful git commit in Phase 8.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not a regression — gap in spec, no prior implementation to bisect against

component_chain.role: Step 5: writes single-use commit grant before dispatching changelog-analyst
component_chain.evidence: Read commands/commit.md lines 79-94: write-commit-grant.py invoked once for BULK=false

component_chain.role: Detects nothing_to_commit_precommitted via three-step SHA-stable check; currently returns status with no recovery
component_chain.evidence: Read agents/changelog-analyst.md Structured Final Status Output section

component_chain.role: _evaluate_commit: validates and consumes commit grant on git commit Bash call; NOT called when working tree is clean
component_chain.evidence: Read hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py lines 272-297, 546-589: _lock_grant_for_posttool only called on live commit path

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: DO NOT rule 7: Never overwrite another session's push-gate token
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Prevents session collision on push-gate token write

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: DO NOT rule 12: Never use commit messages matching heredoc form
DO NOT rule 14: Never create bulk-commit sentinels
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Prevents changelog-analyst from self-authorizing bulk commits

development_approach.strategy: Additive: insert a new 'Recovery path when nothing_to_commit_precommitted detected' subsection in agents/changelog-analyst.md immediately after the THREE-STEP SHA-STABLE CHECK, replacing the 'return nothing_to_commit_precommitted' with a recovery commit + push-gate write. Extend the scan from HEAD-only to baseline_head_sha..HEAD range. In commands/commit.md Step 5 (BULK=false only), add a second write-commit-grant.py invocation to cover multi-repo recovery. No other files changed.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA verifies ACs 1-8 by reading the modified spec prose and checking that the recovery path is self-consistent with existing DO NOT rules, grant lifecycle, and push-gate token write logic. No live git session needed; agent-prompt spec verification is static.

research_findings.reason: Straightforward additive extension to existing well-documented spec pattern; no new design pattern or architecture decision required

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex exec was blocked by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py (the prompt text contained 'git commit' substring which triggered the guard's pattern matching on the Bash command). Self-review substituted.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review substituted. Self-review questions addressed: (1) Over-engineering? No — spec changes are minimal: one new subsection in changelog-analyst.md + one extra grant write in commit.md Step 5. (2) Missed edges? Multi-repo handled explicitly. Grant availability after working-tree-clean analysis documented. Range scan for multi-commit bulk sweeps specified. (3) Regression risk? AC8 explicitly guards non-precommitted path. (4) Scope drift? Confirmed additive-only; no BULK=true changes, no script changes, no schema changes. (5) /close debate readiness? Yes — push-gate token write is the verifiable close criterion.
